rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND TOWNSEND et al VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 20, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Brown Sheet Metal & Mechanical, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. The Court considered defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication as defendant's separate statement complied with CRC 3.1350. As to summary judgment and summary adjudication Issues One and Three (negligence), defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Raymond Townsend was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's failure to address decedent's deposition testimony and to address properly identified person with knowledge Charlie Hedden precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Decedent's deposition testimony would be and is admissible because decedent is unavailable, defendant was a party to decedent's personal injury action and defendant had the right and opportunity to cross-examine decedent with an interest and motive similar to that which it has in this action. (Evid. Code § 1291.) Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Plaintiffs' objections to Michael Brown's declaration are overruled as to paragraphs 1 and 2 and sustained as to paragraph 10. However even if Mr. Brown's declaration was entirely admissible, it would be insufficient to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claim. As to summary adjudication Issues Two and Four (strict liability), defendant's separate statement fails to demonstrate any facts relevant to these causes of action and thus defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. As to summary adjudication Issue Five (premise owner/contractor liability), defendant's separate statement fails to demonstrate any facts to support the ground upon which defendant moved, i.e., that there's no evidence that defendant retained control over decedent's job site, and thus defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Further, defendant failed to demonstrate that this cause of action fails even if defendant did not retain control over decedent's job site. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
